title: Coffee on Carpet
stainChemistry: Coffee on carpet is a tannin stain (polyphenols + melanoidins) with the same carpet-specific construction risks as red wine: pooled liquid soaks into the backing and pad, wicks back up as the carpet dries. Light coffee + cream is actually worse than black on carpet because the milk protein + fat add a combination-stain layer. If the coffee had cream, the protein chemistry applies AS WELL — see coffee-cream-cotton's protein-before-heat logic adapted for carpet here. No alkaline cleaners on tannin.
whyThisWorks: Same extraction pattern as red-wine-carpet. Damp-cloth transfer (never pour), weighted dry towel between passes protects the pad. Acid-side tannin spotter + 3% H₂O₂ on residual color. For coffee WITH cream, an enzyme damp cloth step is inserted before the acid spotter to digest the milk protein.
defaultAssumption: Assume synthetic face yarn. Wool carpet + coffee = pro-lane. Coffee-with-cream version is combination-stain chemistry; this card covers both the black and with-cream paths.

spottingProtocol.technique: Press dry, lift, repeat — no rubbing
spottingProtocol.equipment: Clean white cotton towels, firm pressure
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press a folded white towel straight down with firm pressure. Lift, dry section, press again. Outside-in. Never rub.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD in cold water, damp cloth, weighted dry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply cold NSD via damp cloth. Press, lift, rotate. Never pour. Extract between passes with weighted dry towel. 2–4 cycles.

spottingProtocol.technique: Damp cloth enzyme for milk protein
spottingProtocol.equipment: Liquid Protein Spotter in warm water (30–38°C) OR Protein enzyme pre-soak, damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: For coffee-WITH-cream specifically: damp cloth with enzyme solution pressed onto the stain. Protease digests milk protein before the tannin chemistry runs. Skip for plain black coffee.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side tannin spotter by dropper on residual brown. Dwell 5–10 min, blot with dry towel. Patch-test first on dyed / patterned carpet.
3% H₂O₂ drop-by-drop on residual chromophore. Check every 5 min. Blot when color breaks. One oxidizer at a time.

spottingProtocol.equipment: NSD damp cloth, heavy dry towel + weight
spottingProtocol.instruction: NSD-damp cloth + weighted dry towel 30 min. Pull moisture up from backing.
Fan, air-dry, inspect next day. Wicking back = pad contamination = pro.

steps: 2. Cold NSD via damp cloth + weighted extract between every pass.
3. Coffee WITH cream: insert enzyme damp-cloth step for milk protein.
4. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A).
6. NSD-damp flush + 30 min weighted dry towel.
7. Fan-dry. Inspect next day for wicking.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
warnings: Never pour or pool liquid on carpet
Never use hot water — sets tannin + activates backing adhesive
Never alkaline on coffee tannin — locks as permanent brown
Coffee-with-cream adds enzyme step — protein before tannin chemistry

homeSolutions.instruction: Find a hidden patch of carpet — inside a closet or under furniture. Dab a drop of cool water + Dawn. If carpet dye lifts onto a white cloth, home chemistry may lighten your carpet. Stop and call a pro.
Press a thick stack of white paper towels or folded white cloth straight down on the spill. Lift, dry section, press. Outside-in. Never rub — rubbing drives coffee into the pad.

homeSolutions.agent: Coffee With Cream? Enzyme Step First
homeSolutions.instruction: If the coffee had milk or cream: dissolve an enzyme laundry pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz) in WARM water per the label. Dampen a clean white cloth with that solution, press on the spot, let it sit 10 minutes. This handles the milk protein — skipping this step and going straight to peroxide locks the protein in.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water + Dawn on a DAMP Cloth
homeSolutions.instruction: Dampen a white cloth with cool water + a drop of Dawn. Press on the spot, lift, rotate. Never pour water on carpet — it goes to the pad.
Mix 1 tbsp white vinegar in 1 cup cool water. Dab on residual brown with a damp cloth, let sit 3–5 min, blot dry.

For a stubborn brown shadow on light carpet: mix 1 tbsp Dawn with 1 cup 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide. Dip a cloth, wring nearly dry, dab. Dwell 5 min, blot with damp cloth. Skip on dark / patterned carpet.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia. Patch test on colored carpet first.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Dry Towel Between Every Wet Pass
homeSolutions.instruction: After each wet cycle, press a thick DRY towel + heavy book on the spot for 20–30 min. Pulls moisture UP out of the pad. Non-negotiable on carpet.

homeSolutions.agent: Fan-Dry, Inspect Next Day, Escalate If Needed
homeSolutions.instruction: Fan the area. Don't walk on it until bone-dry. Next day, check for wicking. Escalate for wool carpet, wicking ring, patch-test failure, or two home rounds without clearing. Tell them: "Coffee on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet — [black / with cream], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with [enzyme + ] Dawn + vinegar + 3% peroxide + weighted towel."

steps: 3. Coffee WITH cream: enzyme pretreat damp cloth first (protein step).
4. Cold water + Dawn on DAMP cloth — never pour.
6. 3% peroxide + Dawn mix for stubborn shadow (light carpet only, patch-test).
7. Weighted dry towel 20–30 min between wet passes.
8. Fan-dry. Inspect next day. Escalate for wool, wicking, or plateau.
products: Heavy book or pan for weighted drying
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop for wool carpet, stain wicks back, patch-test failure, or two-round plateau.
warnings: NEVER alkaline on tannin — permanent brown
Coffee with cream needs enzyme step first (protein)
Skip peroxide on dark / patterned carpet

fiberSensitivities: Nylon / olefin / polyester: tolerates standard chemistry

materialWarnings: Coffee with cream = enzyme step first

escalation.when: Wool carpet, stain wicks back, patch-test fails, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Coffee on [nylon / wool / synthetic] carpet — [black / with cream], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with [enzyme + ] Dawn + vinegar + 3% H₂O₂ + weighted towel."

contextQuestions: Carpet fiber — nylon / olefin / polyester / wool?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Coffee-with-cream on carpet = combination-stain + pad-risk. Enzyme step has to come first, then the tannin sequence. Skipping protein-before-heat on carpet-with-cream locks everything.
